title: Coordinate Compass
short_description: Read the map grid and sail to the right square — from friendly letter-number squares, to (x, y) coordinates, to four-quadrant maps that cross the equator and prime meridian.
description: Captain Compass needs you at the helm: an order comes in — 'sail to C4', 'plot the island at (4, 2)', 'the treasure is at (−3, 2)' — and you tap the matching square on the map grid. It teaches the coordinate skills real maps use: reading a grid reference, plotting ordered pairs, and then the big idea that latitude and longitude run both ways from the equator and prime meridian, splitting the world into north/south and east/west. Grade bands ramp from a 5×5 letter-number grid, to a first-quadrant (x, y) grid, to a four-quadrant grid with negative coordinates and hemispheres. Fast, accurate sailing and streaks earn bonus points. A free online map grid and coordinates game for kids in grades 1–5, feeding your score and GP on OnlyGrins.
controls: Mouse / tap the grid square that matches the coordinate.
